Title: Data Science Officer
Description:
Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers! We are a leader among energy companies in North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer service - while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.




Great Benefits

Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
Defined-benefit pension plan for long-term financial security.
Nine-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and community.




Position Overview:

Reporting to the Enterprise Data & Analytics Lead, the Data Science Officer is responsible for planning, executing, and delivering data science and advanced analytics solutions by modeling complex business problems through statistical, algorithmic, mining, and visualization techniques. The focus of this role will be in machine learning (ML), artificial intelligence (AI), modelling and associated data solution development, utilizing and enabling hypothesis-based problem analysis, data exploration and preparation, data collection and integration, and operationalization for both everyday AI (optimization) and future AI (innovation). Further, this role will support business leaders by creating business insights, reports, and analyses to aid in the decision-making process.




The Data Science Officer is expected to be visionary and strategic with an ability to turn innovative ideas for the utility industry into real business value through an open mindset. The Data Science Officer also provides technical leadership in the development of enterprise policies and strategies to leverage ML and AI to achieve business outcomes. There is a clear expectation that ML and AI solutions will be developed and deployed using explainable, responsible and ethical practices to ensure these technologies are used appropriately and to effectively manage associated risks and biases. Success also hinges on the ability to collaborate effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders including other Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Data Developers, Data Architects, Enterprise Architecture, Cyber Security, and Cloud Platform teams, to design and implement robust, secure, data science and advanced analytics solutions.




Responsibilities:

Designs and conducts data analyses with the highest standard of rigor and scientific accuracy, including study design, methodology, algorithms, and statistical modeling
Performs large-scale experimentation and builds data-driven models to identify hidden relationships between variables in large datasets and to answer business questions
Proactively mines data sources to identify trends and patterns and generates insights for business stakeholders and senior leadership
Leads projects that implement complex ML and AI solutions, leveraging complex and advanced tools and techniques
Anticipates internal customers' short and long-term needs by proactively identifying Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) opportunities; innovates for utility industry solutions and shares these opportunities with business stakeholders to validate relevance and value
Collaborates with business stakeholders, providing consultative advice and expertise to translate complex business needs into analytics requirements to support business decisions
Develops and maintains strong working relationships while growing utility industry knowledge to assess longer-term more strategic needs of internal customers across the organization
Grows knowledge through developing working relationships with industry and academic contacts to research classical and cutting-edge techniques and tools in machine learning, deep learning, artificial intelligence, statistical analysis, and visualization techniques and keep abreast of industry best practices
Documents and mentors team members in guidelines and standards, ensuring process alignment, shares technical expertise, provides training, performs code reviews, directs the execution of their tasks, and provides feedback on their technical performance
Partners with D&T and business stakeholders to develop ML and AI policies and strategies
Delivers formal presentations to internal business stakeholders at various levels including executives




Qualifications:

A four year degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, Applied Mathematics, or a related quantitative field from a university of recognized standing with a minimum of five years general IT experience, including three years of directly applicable Data & Analytics programming experience launching, planning, and executing data science projects, including statistical analysis, data engineering, and data visualization

Or

A two year diploma in Data Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related quantitative field with a minimum of seven years general IT experience, including three years of directly applicable Data & Analytics programming experience launching, planning, and executing data science projects, including statistical analysis, data engineering, and data visualization

Or

Alternate experience and education in equivalent areas such as economics, engineering, or physics is acceptable; experience in more than one area is strongly preferred
A specialization in ML, AI, cognitive science or data science is preferred
Microsoft and/or Databricks certifications in AI and ML preferred
Fluency in multiple programming languages and statistical analysis tools such as Python, Jupyter Notebook, C++, JavaScript, R, Scala, SAS, Excel, SQL, MATLAB, SPSS
Experience with relational database programming languages including SQL and PL/SQL as well as nonrelational databases such as NoSQL/Hadoop-oriented databases including MongoDB, Cassandra, etc.
Knowledge of distributed data/computing tools such as Spark, MapReduce, Hadoop, Hive, or Kafka
Experience working across multiple deployment environments including cloud, on-premises, and hybrid, and multiple operating systems and containerization techniques such as Docker, Kubernetes, Azure, etc.
Strong understanding of AI domains such as ML, Generative AI, Optimization, Graphs, and Simulation, and their potential roles in solving business problems such as prediction/forecasting, planning, computer vision, recommendation, natural language processing, content generation, and knowledge discovery.
Experience in one or more of the following commercial/open-source data discovery/analysis platforms: RStudio, Spark, KNIME, RapidMiner, Alteryx, Dataiku, H2O, SAS Enterprise Miner (SAS EM) and/or SAS Visual Data Mining and Machine Learning, Microsoft AzureML, Databricks, IBM Watson Studio or SPSS Modeler, Amazon SageMaker, Google Cloud ML, SAP Predictive Analytics
Experience in statistical and data mining techniques such as generalized linear model (GLM)/regression, random forest, boosting, trees, text mining, hierarchical clustering, deep learning, convolutional neural network (CNN), recurrent neural network (RNN), T-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE), graph analysis, etc.
Experience in applying DevOps/MLOps methods to the construction of ML and data science pipelines
Knowledge of Responsible AI with demonstrated experience aligning to Responsible AI best practices
Understanding of Data Privacy regulations and best practices
Experience in DevOps and Agile (Scrum/Kanban), preferred
Willingness and ability to learn new technologies on the job
Ability to communicate complex projects, models, and results to a diverse audiences with a wide range of technical and non-technical understanding
Ability to work in diverse, cross-functional teams in a dynamic business environment
Good presentation skills, including storytelling and other techniques to guide and inspire
Ability to create relationships quickly and strengthen relationships confidently
Demonstrated ability to be the technical lead on multiple projects or activities with competing priorities




Salary Range

Starting salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The range for the classification is $47.22-$65.19 Hourly, $90,484.68-$124,925.58 Annually.




Apply Now!

Ready to join a team that energizes Manitoba and puts safety, innovation, and inclusion at the heart of everything we do? Visit www.hydro.mb.ca/careers to learn more about this position and to apply online.




Application deadline: JULY 21, 2026.




We appreciate your interest in Manitoba Hydro and thank all applicants. Only those selected for the next stage of the selection process will be contacted.




If you require accommodations during the recruitment process or need this posting in an accessible format, please let us know - we're committed to a barrier-free experience for all candidates.

Title: Junior Data Scientist
Description:
About XTEL

XTEL is a leading global revenue management solutions provider for consumer goods companies (CPG). We help our clients' businesses grow smarter, every day, through our holistic and proven digitalisation and change management approach. By combining state-of-the-art technology, deep industry expertise, and a proven approach to data and change management, XTEL's Revenue Management suite supports CPGs towards value realisation and profitable revenue growth.




Position Overview

The Junior Data Scientist supports the design and delivery of analytics and machine learning solutions by working with complex datasets, building tailored models, and contributing to scalable data pipelines. The role collaborates closely with technical, functional, product, and client stakeholders to develop robust solutions and help standardize analytics practices across projects.




Key Responsibilities

Analyze and preprocess complex data sets from various sources to prepare them for analytics solutions.
Develop, implement, and fine-tune machine learning models tailored to specific project needs.
Collaborate with the team to build robust and scalable data pipelines.
Participate in client meetings to discuss technical results and gather feedback.
Work together with the functional team involved in the project, including business experts and the project manager.
Work together with the product team to integrate developed solutions into our products.
Document processes and contribute to the standardization of analytics solutions.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in STEM fields, such as Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, or related disciplines.
1-2 years of work experience in Data Science and Analytics.
Knowledge for Data Science, including Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and Operations Research.
knowledge of Python and main Machine Learning frameworks, such as scikit-learn, PyTorch, pandas, and NumPy.
Experience with object-oriented programming.
Experience in developing, validating, and tuning Machine Learning models.
Experience with cloud services to store data, build automated pipelines, and run analytics or machine learning solutions.
Experience with Databricks.
Familiarity with development tools such as VSCode or other IDEs, and Jupyter notebooks.
Knowledge of Git, including push, pull, fetch, and merge.
Ability to work in multidisciplinary teams and communicate complex results clearly.
Analytical skills with a critical thinking attitude.
Proficient in English.




A great candidate will have:

Nice to have:

CPG / FMCG industry knowledge is an advantage.
Experience with SQL for data manipulation and analysis.




Geographical Location:

Belgium (Leuven — Hybrid)
Hybrid work arrangements




What We Offer

Hybrid working set-up;🏡
Flexible working hours;⌚
Competitive Salary Package and Bonus scheme;💸
A challenging role in a fast-growing AI-driven company; 🪄
A diverse and international team with strong ownership and a can-do mentality.🌏
Opportunities to contribute meaningfully to the organization’s growth and development.🚀




Equal Opportunities Statement

If you have strengths to share, we’d love to hear from you. We value diverse backgrounds and experiences, so don’t hesitate to apply even if you don’t meet all criteria.

XTEL is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment.

Title: Data Scientist - Internship
Description:
Job Description



The CRM Data Scientist is responsible for analyzing large quantities of information and data (even unstructured) in order to propose models that will improve the understanding of phenomena relating to customers and the business in general (product, retail, communications, style). The CRM Data Scientist has, among its objectives, those of: modeling complex anthropological issues, identifying relevant and pertinent findings and insights through the use of statistical, algorithmic, data extraction and visualization techniques; integration and preparation of large and varied data sets, designing targeted databases, computing environments consistent with the analysis purposes and communicating the results. The CRM Scientist also manages ad hoc projects to identify the models to be used to test hypotheses and carry out data mining, as well as to establish the validity of new relationship marketing activities towards customers and determine the value, over time, of the practices launched in the different market segments. Reports directly to the CRM Director





Qualifications



1.01 Designs and carries out quantitative analyzes that translate into actionable insights in terms of customer relational marketing actions

1.02 Knows and understands the logic of customer acquisition and engagement and the related analytical funnel

1.03 Develops new knowledge from the analysis of the data it uses to inspire decisions and drive business

1.04 Presents data and analyzes effectively to internal stakeholders

1.05 Capitalizes on the use of insights, findings, datasets and statistical models for various business needs

1.06 Deals with the pre-processing of more adequate analysis structure with respect to the objectives set

1.07 Uses machine learning tools (CLTV/Propensity) and statistical techniques to produce solutions to emerging problems

1.08 Carry out hypothesis tests and research from which to develop analysis prototypes

1.09 Keeps updated on new tools and techniques of data analysis

1.10 Promotes the development of training/learning of the Role through self, digital and collaborative learning activities aimed at innovation and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of one's Responsibilities

1.11 Promotes, adopts and develops the use of technologies digital within its function in relation to both the management of the process and in relations with internal and external interlocutors involved in the business

1.12 Adopts significant behaviors and promotes actions linked to Sustainable Development, understood as the interconnection of practices oriented towards Environmental Care , of Business Processes and of the Organizational Community





Additional Information



Requirements:



- Degree in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a STEM field.



- knowledge of Python and SQL



- basic knowledge of data visualization tools



- knowledge of Big Query

Title: Head of AI & Data Science
Description:
Head of AI & Data Science | Enterprise AI Transformation | Cologne (Hybrid)

Location: Cologne (Hybrid)

Package: Flexible depending on experience and impact




AI Futures have been retained to identify a Head of AI & Data Science for a major organisation embarking on the next phase of its enterprise AI transformation.

This is an opportunity to lead the development of AI and Data Science capabilities across a large international business, shaping both the strategic direction and technical delivery of AI initiatives.




You'll play a pivotal role in defining how Artificial Intelligence is adopted at scale, leading high-performing teams and partnering with executive stakeholders to drive measurable business impact.




The Role

Define and execute the organisation's AI & Data Science strategy.
Build, lead and develop high-performing AI Engineering, Data Science and Machine Learning teams.
Drive the adoption of Generative AI, LLMs and intelligent automation across multiple business functions.
Establish scalable AI platforms, governance frameworks and best practices.
Partner with executive stakeholders to identify and prioritise high-value AI opportunities.
Lead the delivery of enterprise AI initiatives from concept through to production.
Develop long-term capability across AI Engineering, Data Science and MLOps.
Influence technology strategy, innovation and AI investment across the organisation.




The Candidate

Significant leadership experience within AI, Data Science or Machine Learning.
Proven experience building and scaling technical teams.
Strong understanding of Generative AI, LLMs, AI platforms and modern Machine Learning.
Experience delivering enterprise AI programmes with measurable business outcomes.
Strong commercial awareness with the ability to bridge business and technology.
Excellent leadership, stakeholder management and communication skills.
Comfortable operating within complex enterprise environments.
Fluent German and English.




Desirable Experience

Enterprise AI transformation.
AI Platform Engineering and MLOps.
Agentic AI and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
AI Governance and Responsible AI.
Azure AI, AWS or GCP.
Data Science platform strategy.
Executive engagement and organisational change leadership.




Why Join?

Lead one of Germany's most ambitious AI transformation programmes.
Shape the long-term AI & Data Science strategy of a major enterprise.
Build and develop a world-class AI organisation.
Work closely with executive leadership on business-critical initiatives.
Significant influence over technology, people and innovation.
Join a business making substantial long-term investment in Artificial Intelligence.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
At Gauss & Neumann, we are looking for PhDs, MSc and BS mathematics, physics and engineering to work with us in our office in Andorra or work remotely. We do Search Engine Marketing (SEM) worldwide and work for international clients. We love and breath technology and everyone at the company has a tech background.

We believe that advertising on search engines (SEM) should provide perfect answers to the users. Gauss & Neumann creates data structures and technologies that help our clients deliver a perfect response to queries and provide value to search engine customers like Google or Bing.




What we look for:

Professionals with a PhD, MSc or bachelor degree in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, Software Engineering, or other engineering disciplines. We search for natural problem solvers with initiative that push the needle always a bit further and have the self-discipline to get things done. We look for people that have fun at work and create a great atmosphere with their teammates. We value personal attitude at the very top.




Responsibilities:

Design SEM keyword structures for international clients

Create predictive algorithms for bidding in real time

Create scripts for optimizing keyword structures

Create technologies that disrupt the SEM industry

Data research and visualization

Manage campaign daily operations with large clients mainly in the United States.




What you need to have:

Bachelor, MSc or PhD level degree in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science or

Software Engineering or similar engineering degree.

Programming skills (Python, SQL, PHP, R...).

Statistics knowledge




Other skills we value:

English fluency (written and verbal)

Project management experience

Natural ability to deal with ratios and numbers

Being up to date with programming libraries

Willingness to learn and go beyond what's necessary.




Why joining Gauss & Neumann:

Gauss & Neumann provides an open environment of excellence with some of the best SEM

professionals in the advertising industry. Most of our staff holds a PhD in Mathematics or

Physics. G&N open culture provides unlimited vacation time, total daily autonomy and the ability to run your own projects and take relevant decisions within the company.




Location:

We are looking for professionals to join our teams in Barcelona and Andorra or to work 100% remotely.

Title: Data Scientist
HTML:
A Company you will be happy to grow with!

At InnoTech, it’s all about connecting people with business through technology.

We truly care about you, your happiness, and your well-being. We look for passionate people who are eager to learn, evolve, and grow with us - because growth is in our DNA.

This commitment has been consistently recognized: we are proud to be a Great Place to Work for five consecutive years, to be one of the Best IT Companies in Portugal, and to be recognized as a Best Workplace Wellbeing.

We are also proud to hold ISO 27001 and ISO 9001 certifications, reinforcing our commitment to information security, quality, and continuous improvement.

For us, sustainability is not just a buzzword. Integrity, social responsability, and people-first values guide everything we do.

Join our team of 180+ InnoTechers and bring your talent as a Data Scientist.


Your Mission Will Be

  • Analyze and understand client datasets to ensure they are ready for advanced analytical solutions;
  • Prepare, cleanse, validate, and transform data using programming languages and scripting tools to support model development;
  • Build, optimize, and refine machine learning models by engineering features and adjusting parameters to improve predictive performance;
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to enhance methodologies, processes, best practices, and data science tooling.


You Will Need

  • MSc or PhD in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Physics, or a related field, together with solid knowledge of machine learning principles and model evaluation techniques;
  • Hands-on experience with Python, Bash, and either Java or Scala, as well as familiarity with Big Data technologies such as Spark and Hadoop;
  • Understanding of resource monitoring, runtime optimization, and performance tuning at both operating system and JVM levels;
  • CPLP nationality or an EU work permit is required to facilitate employment.


Would Be Awesome If You Have

  • Experience with statistical analysis and data visualization techniques;
  • Knowledge of tree-based machine learning algorithms such as Random Forest, XGBoost, or LightGBM;
  • Familiarity with Deep Learning concepts and frameworks;
  • Previous experience working in collaborative and cross-functional data science environments.


What We Offer

  • Health-focused partnerships, including free mental health support;
  • 25 days of vacation, including your birthday, Carnival, and a bridging day between Christmas and New Year;
  • Half a vacation day on your children's birthday and a school supplies voucher;
  • Petsitting partnerships and a pet bereavement day;
  • Regular team buildings, four annual company parties, game nights, and social events;
  • InnoSports initiatives (football, marathons, and more);
  • Events focused on sustainability and social responsability actions.

Are you convinced? Come see it for yourself.

Join our Top-Notch Team!

Title: Machine Learning Engineer (m/f/d)
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer (m/f/d)




💰 Salary: €90,000–€120,000 gross per year (depending on experience)

📍 Location: Remote within Germany




About the Role

We're looking for an experienced Machine Learning Engineer to join an AI-driven company building production-ready machine learning systems at scale.

You'll take ownership of the entire ML lifecycle—from prototyping and evaluation to deployment and production—working on challenging applications across NLP, speech, computer vision and generative AI. You'll collaborate closely with Data Scientists, Data Engineers and DevOps teams to build reliable, scalable solutions that create real business impact.




Your Responsibilities

Build end-to-end machine learning systems, from data ingestion to production deployment
Develop and deploy ML models into production environments
Design scalable inference pipelines using modern serving frameworks
Create evaluation frameworks with meaningful quality, latency and cost metrics
Implement RAG pipelines using vector databases
Build streaming and real-time analytics pipelines
Optimize models for performance, scalability and infrastructure efficiency
Contribute to MLOps processes including monitoring, A/B testing and lifecycle management
Work closely with cross-functional engineering teams to bring AI solutions into production




What We're Looking For

✔ 3+ years of experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or in a similar role

✔ Strong Python skills with hands-on experience in PyTorch and/or TensorFlow

✔ Proven experience deploying ML models into production

✔ Docker and Kubernetes experience

✔ Experience building API-based ML services and CI/CD pipelines

✔ Strong understanding of MLOps, monitoring and experiment tracking

✔ Ability to build reliable, scalable and production-ready ML systems

✔ Based in Germany with valid work authorization




Nice to Have

Experience with LLMs and RAG systems
Triton, ONNX, TensorRT or CUDA
Kafka, Spark, Ray, Flink or Beam
MLflow or similar MLOps tools
Experience in regulated or high-security environments
German language skills (B1+)




What You'll Get

✅ Competitive salary between €90,000 and €120,000

✅ 100% remote within Germany

✅ Work on production-grade AI systems with real-world impact

✅ Modern ML stack, GPU infrastructure and cutting-edge tooling

✅ High ownership and visibility across engineering teams

✅ Opportunity to shape ML and MLOps best practices

✅ 30 days of annual leave plus learning budget and equipment




Interested?

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Who We Are

At JD.com, also known as JINGDONG, we are building one of the world’s most trusted technology and supply chain ecosystems. What started as a bold idea in China has evolved into a NASDAQ-listed, Fortune 44 global enterprise serving over 700 million active customers. Powered by advanced logistics, innovative technology, and a commitment to integrity, JD.com operates across retail, logistics, technology, property and more, creating solutions that make everyday life smarter, faster, and more connected.

Our Global Reach

JD.com is expanding its international footprint through a digitally intelligent, cross-border supply chain and world-class logistics network, supported by platforms such as Joybuy, which brings trusted products to customers worldwide. Today, our teams operate across China, the U.S., U.K., Netherlands, France, Germany, Spain, Brazil, Hungary, Japan, South Korea, Australia, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia, Indonesia, Saudi Arabia, the UAE and beyond, reaching customers in every corner of the world.

Learn more about who we are and what we do: https://corporate.jd.com/

What We Offer

Joining JD.com means becoming part of a company where people, innovation, and impact go hand in hand. We offer a competitive salary, a great location, and a supportive, people-first culture where your growth truly matters. With access to global projects, learning opportunities, and an inclusive environment that values every voice, you’ll have the space to shape the future with us.

About The Team

You’ll be joining the JD.com team, which values collaboration, ownership, and a commitment to delivering high-quality results in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

JD.com is seeking a Data Scientist to develop data-driven insights and machine learning models that power our personalization, recommendation, and analytics systems. In this role, you will transform raw data into business intelligence, working closely with data engineers, product managers, and business teams to improve decision-making and enhance customer experience across JD.com’s international e-commerce operations. Learn more: https://corporate.jd.com/

Key Responsibilities


Build predictive models and advanced analytics solutions to improve personalization, recommendation, and customer segmentation.
Analyze large-scale datasets to identify behavioral trends, business opportunities, and product improvement areas.
Partner with data engineers to ensure clean, reliable, and accessible data for experimentation and model deployment.
Design and run A/B tests to measure the impact of algorithms and product features.
Translate technical insights into clear, actionable recommendations for business and product stakeholders.

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
💡 OVENTI Consulting, cabinet de conseil en Data et Intelligence Artificielle qui allie expertise technique de pointe, vision stratégique et accompagnement sur mesure. Nous couvrons tout le cycle de vie des projets Data/IA, de la stratégie à l'implémentation de solutions personnalisées (ML, BI, automatisation intelligente).

Nos équipes, issues des meilleures écoles, s'intègrent chez nos clients ou opèrent depuis notre LAB interne, véritable foyer d’innovation. Chez Oventi, nous privilégions le "sur mesure" et l'innovation concrète. Chaque collaborateur est accompagné, formé et suivi pour garantir une culture d'excellence et un impact réel.







🧑‍💻 Pourquoi ce rôle est clé ?

En tant que Data Scientist H/F, vous intervenez sur l'exploration, le test et l'industrialisation de l'IA Générative de notre client au niveau national.




🎯 Vos missions au quotidien :

Développement et expérimentation de solutions en IA Générative :

LLMs, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), multimodalité et IA conversationnelle.
Tester et comparer les technologies du marché en fonction des cas d’usage.
Développer des POCs robustes, validés par l’impact métier.
Analyser et structurer les données d’entraînement pour garantir la performance des modèles.




De l'expérimentation à l'industrialisation :

Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les autres équipes intervenants sur les sujets AI Gen pour assurer la mise en production des solutions IA validées.
Définir les pipelines MLOps et CI/CD adaptés aux contraintes des modèles génératifs.
Optimiser la scalabilité et le monitoring des modèles une fois en production.
Collaborer avec les équipes DevOps et développement logiciel pour intégrer les modèles IA dans les produits métiers.
Collaboration avec des profils techniques et fonctionnels de haut niveau : architectes, développeurs, product managers, data governance manager, pour ne citer qu’eux.




Veille et innovation continue :

Suivre les dernières avancées en IA Générative et proposer des innovations adaptées aux métiers.
Assurer une veille technologique active sur les outils de Data Science et leur intégration dans l'écosystème.







🧩 Vous êtes la personne idéale si :

7 ans d'expérience en Data Science y compris avec des sujets d'IA Générative.
Maîtrise des LLMs et architectures Transformer, expérience en fine-tuning, RAG, vector databases etc...
Solide expérience en MLOps et industrialisation de modèles IA.




🧩 Stack Technique :

Langage de programmation : Python, C#, VueJS, react, SQL, MDX, DAX
Librairies : Pytorch, Langchain, Yolo
Cloud : GCP
LLM : OpenAI, Mistral, LLAMA, Claude
Database : SQL, CosmoDB
Outil de Dev : Visual Studio, Visual Code, Azure DevOps, Jira, Github
Ops : CICD, MLFlow, PyTorch







🌟 Pourquoi rejoindre OVENTI ?

Rejoindre Oventi, c'est choisir une aventure professionnelle unique :




✔️Une structure à taille humaine : Favorisant l'échange, l'autonomie et l'innovation, où votre impact est direct et visible.

✔️Des missions à forte valeur ajoutée : Dans des secteurs variés, avec une approche "sur mesure" où nos solutions ont des impacts importants chez nos clients.

✔️Une culture d’excellence technique : Avec un réel accompagnement de carrière et un suivi personnalisé.

✔️Une équipe passionnée et engagée : Des ingénieurs recrutés avec soin, formés en continu, et investis sur le long terme.

✔️L’agilité d’une startup, la rigueur d’un cabinet expert : Le meilleur des deux mondes pour une expérience enrichissante.

✔️Des missions à impact, visibles et valorisantes : Où vous aurez un impact réel sur les décisions métiers et la valeur business générée.







📍 Localisation : Massy (91) avec 2 à 3 jours de télétravail hebdomadaire

💶 Rémunération : Selon profil

🗓️ Démarrage : ASAP

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Who we are. What we do.

We are Bystronic - a global technology company passionate about shaping the future of industrial manufacturing. We want to work with people who support us in creating new possibilities for connected sustainable production worldwide.

As a leading provider of solutions for sheet metal and material processing, we combine laser cutting technology, press brakes, automation, and software with innovative laser applications for new materials and processes.

Bystronic Software. The digital future of sheet metal processing.

In this role, you will join our Bystronic Software team. With our software solutions, developed specifically for the sheet metal processing industry, we enable companies to digitize their entire processes - from the initial quote to the final delivery.

We are looking for a Data Scientist passionate about data, advanced analytics and turning information into strategic decisions. You will work closely with product, engineering and business teams to develop data-driven solutions that create real impact.

What you will be doing

• Analyze large volumes of data to identify patterns, trends and opportunities.

• Design, develop and maintain predictive models and machine learning algorithms.

• Clean, transform and validate data from multiple sources.

• Create dashboards and reports to support business decision-making.

• Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to define metrics and KPIs.

• Present findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.

• Automate analytical processes and data pipelines.

• Explore new techniques and tools related to AI and data science.

Your education & experience

• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Physics, Engineering or a related field.

• 4+ years of experience in Data Science, advanced analytics or similar roles.

• Experience applying statistical modelling, machine learning and data visualization in business contexts.

What you need to succeed

• Strong command of Python or R.

• Experience with libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow or PyTorch.

• Solid SQL knowledge and experience working with databases.

• Experience with data visualization tools such as Qlik, Tableau, Looker or similar.

• Strong understanding of statistics and machine learning.

• Familiarity with Azure cloud environments.

• Analytical thinking, business orientation and strong data storytelling skills.

• Proactive mindset and ability to solve complex problems in collaboration with others.

What sets you apart

• Experience with MLOps.

• Knowledge of NLP or Computer Vision.

• Experience working with Big Data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop or Databricks.

• Excellent English communication skills, both spoken and written.

What's in it for you

• A hybrid work model connected to our offices in Bilbao, Derio and Vitoria.

• Flexible working schedule and reduced working hours during summer.

• Individual learning and development programs, including language courses, Microsoft certifications and participation in technical conferences.

• Multidisciplinary teams with diverse skills, perspectives and backgrounds working in agile ways.

• The opportunity to create real impact in the sheet metal industry and help shape the digital factory of the future.

• Salary and benefits aligned with the market and your experience.

People power our purpose

Imagine working in an environment that helps move the company to the next level, where your passion, values, and skills are integral to impacting industrial manufacturing and beyond. At Bystronic, this can become your reality. Our people are the edge to drive and nurture our ambitions and continued success. Together we have created a culture where our people are central, inspiring us to make decisions that best serve them and are aligned with our purpose. As much as we are in a technology business, human beings drive real change.




Life at Bystronic

Explore our careers page to learn more about how we work:

https://www.bystronic.com/int/en/career/why-join/employee-stories

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Machine Learning/Data Engineer | Antwerp







My client is looking for an experienced ML / Data Engineer to help build intelligent, production-grade machine learning solutions that solve complex real-world problems.

This is an opportunity to join at an growing stage and take ownership of the entire ML lifecycle from data engineering and model development to production deployment and continuous improvement.




The Role-




Build scalable data pipelines that ingest and process large volumes of multi-source data, including tracking data, operational events, and IoT sensor streams.
Design and develop machine learning models for:
Delay prediction
Exception and anomaly detection
Transport plan optimization
Event and milestone forecasting
Create continuous learning pipelines that adapt to changing operational patterns.
Scale ML infrastructure to support near real-time predictions and production workloads.
Collaborate closely with engineering teams to integrate models into live products using testing and monitoring best practices.
Help define the long-term machine learning strategy, architecture, and data roadmap.




Who you are-




4+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in production.
Strong Python skills with experience using frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn.
Hands-on experience building data pipelines, stream processing, and working with graph databases.
Experience designing scalable ML infrastructure and production workflows.
Comfortable working in an environment where you'll help shape the technical direction rather than simply execute predefined tasks.




The Offer-




Clear path of progression
Full package
Competitive salary
Real ownership
Close team




If you're interested in building AI solutions that have a tangible impact and want to work on challenging machine learning problems at scale, I'd love to hear from you.




Please get in touch to learn more or apply.

  • 4+ years of experience building and deploying machine learning models in production.
  • Strong Python skills with experience using frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn.
  • Hands-on experience building data pipelines, stream processing, and working with graph databases.
  • Experience designing scalable ML infrastructure and production workflows.
  • Comfortable working in an environment where you'll help shape the technical direction rather than simply execute predefined tasks.


The Offer-


  • Clear path of progression
  • Full package
  • Competitive salary
  • Real ownership
  • Close team


If you're interested in building AI solutions that have a tangible impact and want to work on challenging machine learning problems at scale, I'd love to hear from you.


Please get in touch to learn more or apply.

Title: Research Scientist (GenAI/LLM)
Description:
We are looking for a Research Scientist with a data-centric mindset to join our applied research and innovation team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning, deep learning, language models, GenAI, AgenticAI and process automation. You will be responsible for analyzing data, researching the most appropriate techniques, and the development, testing, support and delivery of proof of concepts to resolve real world and large-scale challenging problems.





Job Description



Develop and apply machine learning innovations by collaborating with other team members and company stakeholders. Understand the requirements and be able to communicate results and conclusions in a way that is accurate, clear and winsome. Perform feasibility studies and analyse data to determine the most appropriate solution. Work on many different data challenges, always ensuring a combination of simplicity, scalability, reproducibility and maintainability within the ML solutions and source code. Both data and software must be developed and maintained with high-quality standards and minimal defects. Collaborate with other technical fellows on the integration and deployment of ML solutions. To work as a member of a team, encouraging team building, motivation and cultivating effective team relations.



Essential Requirements



Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Maths or an equivalent numerate discipline
Hands-on expertise in Machine Learning, NLP, LLMs, GenAI, AgenticAI and data science
Understanding and practice of prompt engineering, RAG, document processing and fraud detection
EDA analysis and practical hands-on experience with datasets, ML models and evaluations
Python, Pytorch, Git, pandas, dask, polars, sklearn, huggingface, docker, databricks
Analytical mindset, problem solving and logical thinking capabilities
Proactive attitude, constructive, intellectual curiosity and persistence to find answers to questions
A high level of interpersonal and communication skills in English and strong ability to meet deadlines
Keen to work as part of a diverse team of international colleagues and in a global inclusive culture
A minimum of 2-5 years’ experience with evidence in a related ML field


Qualifications



Desired Skills



PhD/Masters in a specific area of AI/ML with published papers
Experience working with large datasets, production-grade code and operationalization of ML solutions
Understanding & practice of LLMs & Generative AI (prompt engineering, RAG)
Previous experience with tabular data, anomaly detection, time series forecasting, automated classification
Experience in MLOPs (MLFlow, Prefect) and deployment of AI/ML solutions to the cloud (esp. Azure)
Previous experience in retail, consumer, ecommerce, business, FMCG products (NielsenIQ portfolio)


Additional Information



Salary: 38,400 to 46,500 EUR gross per year. Placement within the range will depend on objective criteria including experience, skills, and internal equity considerations. We are committed to equal pay and pay transparency. Remuneration decisions are made using gender-neutral and objective criteria.​​



Our Benefits



Flexible working environment
Volunteer time off
LinkedIn Learning
Employee-Assistance-Program (EAP)

These tools are intended to improve efficiency and support fair and consistent evaluation based on job-related criteria. All use of AI is governed by NIQ’s principles of fairness, transparency, human oversight, and inclusion. Final hiring decisions are made exclusively by humans. NIQ regularly reviews its AI tools to help mitigate bias and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations. NIQ is the world’s leading consumer intelligence company, delivering the most complete understanding of consumer buying behavior and revealing new pathways to growth. With a holistic retail read and the most comprehensive consumer insights—delivered with advanced analytics through state-of-the-art platforms—NIQ delivers the Full View™. NIQ is an Advent International portfolio company with operations in 100+ markets, covering more than 90% of the world’s population.

Title: Junior Data Scientist
Description:
Nuestro objetivo es ayudar a todos los recién graduados del mundo a encontrar el trabajo que más se ajuste a sus ambiciones: ¡únete a nosotros para hacer realidad las aspiraciones de una comunidad de más de 1,500,000 usuarios!




Observamos constantemente los recursos de los estudiantes y egresados que aspiran a incorporarse a nuestro equipo de Tecnología: los candidatos para este puesto y nuestro equipo de RRHH se pondrán en contacto contigo sin comunicar puesto y tu CV será el adecuado a tu perfil.




Para estas posiciones junior en el sector Tech, ofrecemos un contrato con una remuneración anual de 24.000€ a 28.000 €, dependiendo de la experiencia. Podrás trabajar en modalidad completamente remota y, por ello, te proporcionaremos en cualquier momento un MacBook de última generación.




¡Nos vamos a España! Si quieres sumarte a crecer en tu país, envía tu CV para este anuncio: si tu perfil está en alguno de nuestros futuros puestos, nos pondremos en contacto contigo ;)

--




Dentro del equipo de Tecnología podrás abordar diversas tareas como:




Implementación de algoritmos de Machine Learning: tendrá la oportunidad de implementar y probar algoritmos de aprendizaje automático, ayudando a desarrollar soluciones inteligentes a los desafíos comerciales. En particular, junto con nuestro equipo tecnológico, trabajarás en Joinrs AI: nuestro modelo de Inteligencia Artificial que ayuda a nuestra comunidad a encontrar trabajo;
Análisis y modelado de datos: participará en el análisis de datos, desarrollará modelos predictivos y ayudará a proporcionar información significativa para mejorar las decisiones comerciales.
Visualización de datos: aprenderá a crear visualizaciones de datos claras e informativas para comunicar de manera efectiva hallazgos complejos a diferentes partes interesadas. En particular, creará paneles interactivos para mostrar mejor los datos y KPI (visualización de datos) al equipo, los clientes, los inversores y todas las partes interesadas clave;
Optimización de Procesos de Negocio: Colaborarás en la identificación y optimización de procesos de negocio mediante la aplicación de soluciones basadas en datos.
Participación en Sesiones de Brainstorming Tecnológico: Participarás en sesiones de Brainstorming para generar ideas innovadoras y contribuirás a la identificación de nuevas oportunidades en el campo de la Inteligencia Artificial.
Formación Continua y Actualización en las Últimas Tendencias: Tendrás acceso a programas de formación continua para mantenerte actualizado sobre las últimas tendencias y avances en el campo de la Ciencia de Datos y la Inteligencia Artificial.

--




Lo que te ofrecemos

Trabajar en el equipo de Joinrs significa poder elegir siempre las mejores condiciones psicofísicas para estar sereno y productivo. Esto se traduce en:




Trabajo remoto y flexibilidad. Tendrás la oportunidad de trabajar completamente a distancia todo el tiempo que desees, pero también apoyarte en una de nuestras oficinas.
Ambiente de trabajo dinámico. Nuestras oficinas siempre están abiertas. Te garantizamos espacios de trabajo compartidos, salas de reuniones, áreas de descanso y un bar con frutas frescas y aperitivos de todo tipo. También encontrarás una mesa de billar y una estación de juegos. Queremos un ambiente de trabajo informal que fomente momentos de compartir, siempre respetando las actividades individuales.
Team building y diversión. Organizamos a menudo aperitivos, juegos y momentos de recreo: son parte integral de la vida en Joinrs, tanto fuera como dentro de la oficina.
Bienestar empresarial. Te garantizamos un bienestar empresarial privado a la altura de tus expectativas.
Paquete tecnológico. Utilizarás software avanzado y tecnologías modernas, y se te proporcionará un MacBook de última generación.
Formación. Siempre contarás con un tutor en tu trayectoria en Joinrs: tendrás un punto de referencia tanto para los aspectos técnicos como para los del equipo. Te aseguramos un crecimiento profesional constante que se traducirá en un aumento progresivo de tus responsabilidades. Podrás participar en eventos del sector y cursos de formación, además de recibir formación continua en las mejores plataformas de aprendizaje en línea.
Impacto social. Tu contribución apoyará a miles de recién graduados a encontrar el trabajo al que aspiran: tendrás la oportunidad de interactuar con nuestra comunidad y descubrir el impacto social y la revolución que estamos llevando más allá de las fronteras italianas.

--




Quiénes somos

Nuestra misión es apoyar a cada recién graduado a lograr sus aspiraciones, permitiéndole comenzar su nueva carrera con el optimismo de una perspectiva clara.




Somos un equipo unido que cree firmemente en el trabajo en equipo: juntos trabajamos, discutimos y obtenemos resultados que nos estimulan a alcanzar objetivos cada vez más ambiciosos.




Nuestra comunidad cuenta con más de 1,500,000 miembros en Italia, Reino Unido, España, Brasil, Francia, Portugal y Alemania, incluyendo 700 empresas como PwC, Accenture, Gi Group, Danone, Siemens, y está creciendo de manera exponencial: ¡únete a nosotros!

--




Contrato y Remuneración:

Para todos los puestos de Junior Data Scientist ofrecemos un contrato con un salari anualo de €24,000 a €28.000.




Se programará una revisión salarial cada seis meses, y tu remuneración crecerá rápidamente a medida que acumules experiencia y la traduzcas en un impacto cada vez mayor.

--




Proceso de selección:

1. Revisión del CV: el primer paso es una revisión del currículum (generalmente dentro de los 20 días posteriores a la solicitud).

2. Entrevista telefónica: el segundo paso incluirá una entrevista telefónica con nuestro Gerente de Recursos Humanos de aproximadamente 15 minutos.

3. Entrevista por video: el último paso será una entrevista por video con tanto el Gerente de Recursos Humanos como tu futuro supervisor. Prevemos al menos 45 minutos de entrevista.

--




¿Por qué unirte a nuestro equipo?

Impacto social: tendrás la oportunidad de apoyar a nuestra comunidad para que logren sus aspiraciones. Y las tuyas.

Startup exitosa: Joinrs fue seleccionada entre las 10 mejores startups del mundo en la Web Summit de Lisboa.

Crecimiento personal exponencial: tendrás la oportunidad de crecer de manera exponencial, tanto en habilidades técnicas como en habilidades blandas, gracias a un programa de formación basado en la capacitación en el trabajo y el apoyo en línea.

Show more

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer (m/f/d)
Description:
We are currently partnering with an innovative technology company to recruit a Senior Machine Learning Engineer / MLOps Engineer. This is an exciting opportunity to join a highly skilled engineering team where you'll help build and scale production-grade machine learning systems that solve complex, real-world challenges using modern AI technologies.




Your Responsibilities

Design, build, and maintain end-to-end machine learning pipelines, from data ingestion and model training through to deployment and monitoring.
Deploy, operate, and optimise machine learning models in production environments, ensuring reliability, scalability, and performance.
Develop scalable data pipelines supporting both batch and real-time machine learning workloads.
Build and improve ML infrastructure, automation, CI/CD processes, and operational tooling.
Collaborate closely with data scientists to productionise machine learning models and translate research into robust production systems.
Partner with software engineers to integrate ML services into larger backend platforms and applications.
Contribute to cloud-native ML platforms using containerisation and orchestration technologies.
Drive continuous improvements to system architecture, observability, performance, and engineering best practices.
Document workflows, infrastructure, and technical solutions while sharing knowledge across the engineering team.




Your Profile

Several years of professional experience developing and operating production machine learning systems.
Strong Python programming skills with experience using frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX.
Experience deploying and managing ML models throughout the complete machine learning lifecycle.
Strong background building scalable data pipelines using technologies such as Apache Spark, Airflow, Prefect, or similar tools.
Experience working with cloud platforms including AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.
Good knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure automation.
Familiarity with distributed systems and designing scalable, reliable software architectures.
Experience collaborating across multidisciplinary engineering teams.
You're ambitious, invested in the company's long-term success, and eager to grow from a senior engineering role into a future technical leadership or management position.
Excellent analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and communication skills.




Ideally, you will also bring experience with one or more of the following:

Distributed training or GPU-accelerated machine learning.
Performance optimisation for model training or inference.
Model monitoring, observability, or drift detection.
Streaming or real-time data processing.
Scientific computing, simulation, or data-intensive applications.
Reinforcement learning, explainable AI, transfer learning, or other advanced ML techniques.




Benefits

Opportunity to work on cutting-edge machine learning solutions with real-world impact.
Join a collaborative team of experienced engineers, data scientists, and technical specialists.
Work with modern cloud-native technologies and industry-leading ML tooling.
Excellent opportunities for professional development and technical growth.
Flexible working environment with a strong engineering culture focused on innovation and continuous improvement.




If you're passionate about building scalable, production-ready machine learning systems and want to make a meaningful impact, we'd love to hear from you.




Location: Remote (within Europe)

Start: ASAP

Salary: ca 85k€

Location: Remote (within Europe)

Start: ASAP

Salary: ca 85k€

Title: Data Scientist | Talent Program
HTML:
Let’s talk talent

You have talent. Ready to make impact. We know. We also know that making impact starts with that first contact between like-minded people who have the same drive to shape their future. We see you, with your skills, personality and dreams. Now let’s take it to even greater heights by means of one of our talent programs for master’s graduates.

We are Mploy Associates, a talent-focused consultancy firm ready to make not only our clients’ teams successful but also you. Your talent, our focus.


More specifically?

We are a consultancy firm specialized in the areas of data, finance, and analytics and work on a variety of projects like investment analyses, data engineering, quant modelling and much more. Our clients are banks, insurance companies, and asset managers and we are still expanding.


We help our clients build successful teams by providing the right skills, talents and know-how at the right place and time and developing ourselves continuously to stay successful. We believe that investing in talent through proven methods always pays out. Today we are proud to say that we are an international-driven company with more than 100 people and many top-tier clients who share the same vision.


How do you fit into this vision?

You will work as a Data Scientist for one of our clients with a special focus on your own development through one of our Talent Programs. Our Talent Programs are designed to accelerate your career from entry-level to medior+ level within 24 months. By means of certified training courses, coaching and intervision sessions our employees follow an above-average steep career curve. Successful graduation in our Talent Programs usually results in a permanent medior+ or senior position at one of our clients or where it becomes your responsibility to keep the team successful.


Your activities as a Data Analyst

From data collection and model development to stakeholder meetings and model calibrations: the activities of a data scientist are highly diverse. Your day-to-day responsibilities include:


  • Developing and refining research questions for further data analysis and modelling;
  • Merging tables and processing data in SQL, SAS and/or Python;
  • Carrying out relevant data quality analyses (e.g. trend, missing value, and extreme value analyses);
  • Interpretation of the data by means of pattern analyses and descriptive statistics;
  • Developing predictive models using common statistical and AI libraries;
  • Monitoring model performance;
  • Calibrating existing models on new datasets;
  • Documenting the modelling process and discussing this with relevant stakeholders.


On average you work 36 hours a week on a client project and you spend 4 hours a week on your own personal, professional and technical development in one of our Talent Programs.


What do we offer?

We offer the opportunity to become an expert in data analysis, in an environment where we train you to excel as a high-end professional. In addition, good working conditions are equally as important to us.


By offering free lunch at the office and regular fun team activities like escape rooms and fun dinners, we ensure a connected and social workplace. Also important; we will provide you with a public transport card and/or (electric) bike. All on us of course. Furthermore:


  • A salary of €3,850 for master's graduates, including holiday allowance and 13th-month salary;
  • Challenging work and projects for one of our top 200 clients;
  • 25 days of paid annual leave;
  • Half-day a week in-house professional and technical training combined with personal coaching;
  • Besides in-house training opportunities, an external development budget of €2,000 per year to obtain relevant certifications as FRM or CFA;
  • An international working environment with driven and ambitious colleagues from all over the world;
  • The opportunity to take your next career move as a medior at the client or Mploy Associates after successfully completing your development program;
  • Pro bono technical projects at socially relevant NGOs.


What are we looking for?

We are looking for graduates with different quantitative and data-related backgrounds, experiences, and passions. It speaks for itself that you are ambitious, self-reflective and dare to be confident in an authentic way.

Furthermore, you are energized by stakeholder management and are well adept at gathering reliable information. You use this in combination with a consultive mindset to make our clients more successful. Last but not least, you are eager to develop yourself on a personal, professional and technical level with our personalized in-house Talent Programs. To make this more specific:


  • You have 0-3 years of work experience and want to learn everything about data science;
  • You want to put into practice your skills that you have acquired during your master's in Data Science, Mathematics, Econometrics or another quantitative and data-related study;
  • You have a strong feel for data and data architecture, as much of the data science craft revolves around creativity and curiosity;
  • You do not hesitate to use your analytical skills to solve complex problems creatively and logically;
  • You work in a structured and precise manner to safeguard reliable results.


Interested?

For more information about our different positions and Talent Programs you can visit our website www.mployassociates.com. Want to know more about the vacancy? Use the apply button above or email us at careers@mployassociates.com from the moment you start your master’s so we can help you prepare for your dream career. We will be in touch.


Please note that unfortunately there is no point in applying when you do not live in the Netherlands or if you have more than 3 years of work experience. These applications will not be processed.


Are you interested in multiple positions at Mploy Associates? Apply for one position and mention the other position(s) in your motivation message. Together we will find the best match for you.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Job description:

Machine Learning Engineer
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer willing to join our Data Science and IoT Platform Team. The role involves building, training, deploying ML models, and collaborating on AI based applications to boost production efficiency and to enable predictive maintenance. It is also significantly involved in our digital transformation process and energy transition journey, offering the opportunity to influence them daily with creativity and innovative strength.
Key Responsibilities:
* Work across the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle, from problem definition and data preparation to model development, deployment, and monitoring
* Build, together with our Software engineers, AI based applications deployed on industrial machinery to boost production efficiency, reduce unplanned downtime and enable predictive maintenance
* Work with colleagues from different Product Lines to help them create and bring innovative solutions to the market

What we ask for:
* STEM Degree (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics)
In depth experience with Python and knowledge of ML libraries (e.g. pandas, sklearn,
keras/ pytorch)
* Hands-on experience developing and deploying machine learning models

* Good Knowledge of English (written and spoken)

Nice to have
* Experience with building and deploying LLM models
* Experience with ML Computer Vision models
* Knowledge of Agile methodology and SCRUM
* Knowledge of Microsoft Azure. We use Synapse and ML Workspace

What we offer:
* The opportunity to contribute to Upstream&TAT - RAID activities with a direct impact on business performance
* A dynamic and international environment where ideas are valued and innovation is encouraged
* Continuous learning opportunities through training, exposure to new technologies and cross-functional collaboration.
* Hybrid working model according to company policy and role requirements
* Performance Management through an annual appraisal and development process
* Company Canteen available on site
* Supplementary healthcare coverage
* Well-being initiatives to support employees


For the position, the starting gross annual salary will be from € 35.000, with the final offer determined based on the candidate's seniority, experience, and skills, as well as the outcome of the selection process.
Workplace: Castellanza (VA)

Title: R&D Data Scientist
Description:
Role Overview

We are looking for a scientific data professional to support the design and development of data solutions for pharmaceutical R&D.

The ideal candidate combines a strong life-sciences or biomedical research background with practical experience working with scientific data, analytical tools, and data models. The role requires extensive collaboration with scientists, business analysts, architects, and data teams.

This is primarily a domain-focused role. The successful candidate should be able to understand scientific workflows and translate them into practical data requirements and solutions, rather than act as a traditional software or data engineer.




Responsibilities

Work with scientific and business stakeholders to understand research workflows, data needs, and existing challenges.
Identify and assess relevant internal and external data sources, data products, systems, and analytical capabilities.
Define data and information flows in collaboration with business analysts and technical teams.
Design scientific data products, including data models, schemas, metadata, and data contracts.
Support the standardization, validation, and integration of scientific data.
Develop prototypes for data ingestion, transformation, validation, and analysis.
Ensure that solutions follow data governance, quality, compliance, and FAIR data principles.
Communicate findings, recommendations, and design decisions to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Explore how data science and AI methods could improve scientific data interpretation and decision-making.




Required Experience

Strong background in life sciences, biomedical research, bioinformatics, computational biology, or a related discipline.
Practical experience working with biomedical, clinical, translational, genomic, manufacturing, or pharmaceutical data.
Understanding of pharmaceutical R&D and the drug development lifecycle.
Experience translating scientific questions into data requirements, analyses, or data solutions.
Knowledge of data modelling, metadata, data quality, data standardization, and FAIR principles.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
Working knowledge of Python and common data analysis tools.




Desirable Experience

Experience working in a pharmaceutical or biotechnology environment.
Familiarity with AWS or another cloud platform.
Experience developing proof-of-concept data pipelines or analytical solutions.
Exposure to machine learning, generative AI, or agentic AI in a biomedical context.
Experience with large-scale genomic, proteomic, imaging, clinical, or longitudinal datasets.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Sobre Fintonic




En Fintonic estamos en pleno renacer. Tenemos más de 12 años de historia como referente del mundo fintech en España, y hoy comenzamos un nuevo capítulo: más ambicioso, más dinámico, más emocionante. ¿El objetivo? Convertirnos en el estándar definitivo del score crediticio y en el principal marketplace de financiación al consumo, gracias a una tecnología superior y un dominio absoluto del uso de los datos.




Nos respaldan inversores institucionales de primer nivel como ING Ventures y SquareOne Capital. Estamos formando un nuevo equipo que no solo se suma al viaje, sino que lo lidera.




💪Tu misión




Buscamos un perfil mid para Fintonic cuya misión será desarrollar soluciones de analítica avanzada y machine learning orientadas al análisis de riesgo, automatización de procesos y mejora continua del negocio.




¿Qué harás?




Desarrollo y mantenimiento del ecosistema de servicios asociados al análisis de riesgo de los usuarios, aplicando técnicas de analítica avanzada y Machine Learning. Esto incluye la implementación de modelos de scoring, categorización de transacciones, detección de comercios, extracción automática de información financiera, detección de fraude, listas negras (blacklists), control de calidad de datos y sistemas de sanción (Wanna).
Brindar soporte diario y resolución de incidencias relacionadas con los sistemas de analítica avanzada, en colaboración con agentes del call center y analistas de riesgo.
Mejora general de procesos de Advanced Analytics




💫Sobre ti




Tienes una titulación en Estadística, Matemáticas, Ingeniería, Informática, Ciencia de Datos o campos afines.
Tienes 2 años de experiencia trabajando en proyectos de ciencia de datos aplicados a negocio.
Tienes experiencia ejecutando iniciativas de machine learning en entornos productivos y colaborando con perfiles multidisciplinares (analistas, ingenieros, producto, soporte).
Aportas experiencia previa en la construcción y mantenimiento de modelos de riesgo, detección de fraude, o motores de scoring (idealmente en fintech o banca digital).
Dominas Python para análisis de datos, machine learning y automatización.
Has utilizado Visual Studio Code como entorno de desarrollo.
Controlas versiones con GitLab
Tienes conocimientos en Docker para contenerización y despliegue de modelos en producción.
Usas servicios en la nube, especialmente AWS.
Trabajas con bases de datos relacionales y no relacionales: MongoDB, Redshift, MySQL.
Tienes conocimientos en visualización de datos, documentación técnica y gestión de tareas: Google Sheets, Confluence, Jira.
Monitoreo de sistemas y rendimiento con Datadog.




Habilidades y Competencias que necesitarás:




Capacidad para traducir necesidades de negocio en soluciones técnicas escalables y sostenibles.
Alta capacidad analítica, orientación a resultados y pensamiento crítico.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación para interactuar con equipos técnicos y no técnicos.
Resolución ágil de incidencias en producción y soporte técnico colaborativo.




Lo que te ofrecemos




Disfrutarás de retribución flexible: tickets restaurante, transporte, seguro médico, guardería, formación
Programas de desarrollo profesional
Clases de inglés
Formación técnica y en habilidades blandas
Ayuda al deporte
24 días de vacaciones + 24 o 31 de diciembre
Seguro de vida una vez superado el periodo de prueba
Y en tu cumple 🎂, tendrás la tarde libre




🚀Conoce nuestros principios y valores




Ganamos la confianza siendo transparentes
Los datos son nuestro superpoder
Hacemos pocas cosas, pero las hacemos excepcionalmente bien
Siempre cumplimos nuestras promesas
Resolvemos las cosas con ingenio




💙 En Fintonic, creemos en el poder de la diversidad. Nuestro equipo está formado por personas de diferentes culturas, trayectorias y formas de ser. No discriminamos por orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género, ni por ninguna otra condición personal. Si compartes nuestros valores, ¡nos encantará conocerte!




Puedes encontrar información sobre cómo procesamos sus datos personales en el siguiente enlace.
